decisions.decision: This repo is cnogo's source AND a cnogo-managed project (self-hosting)
decisions.rationale: The cnogo source repo uses cnogo to manage its own development. After packaging, cnogo is installed into this repo the same way it's installed anywhere else. Development-only files (tests/, install.sh, docs/templates/) sit outside cnogo's installed scope.

decisions.decision: Same installed layout everywhere — source repo and target repos are identical
decisions.rationale: No 'source layout' vs 'installed layout' distinction. The files in git ARE the installable artifact. install.sh copies them as-is. No transformation during install.

decisions.decision: Git-based distribution — install.sh clones/pulls from cnogo repo
decisions.rationale: install.sh fetches from the cnogo git repo (clone for first install, pull for updates). Also supports local path for offline/dev use. The git repo IS the package — no separate build/archive step.

decisions.decision: All cnogo runtime in .cnogo/scripts/ from the start
decisions.rationale: Scripts, memory engine, context graph, hooks all live under .cnogo/. Claude Code integration stays in .claude/ (fixed paths). Clear ownership: if it's in .cnogo/, it's cnogo. No ambiguity with project scripts/ directories.

decisions.decision: Five file ownership categories: managed, seeded, merged, runtime, scaffold
decisions.rationale: Each cnogo file has exactly one ownership category that determines behavior on install/update/uninstall. managed=cnogo owns entirely (overwrite on update, remove on uninstall). seeded=cnogo creates once, user owns after (never overwrite, leave on uninstall). merged=shared ownership with marker-based separation (merge cnogo parts only). runtime=created at runtime, gitignored (recreate if missing, remove on uninstall). scaffold=directory structure (create once, leave on uninstall).

decisions.decision: manifest.json with content hashes and ownership categories
decisions.rationale: Single source of truth for what cnogo installed. Each file entry has category and sha256 hash at install time. Hash enables detecting user modifications to managed files — backup before overwriting on update. Covers files across both .cnogo/ and .claude/.

decisions.decision: settings.json merge via _cnogo marker on hook entries
decisions.rationale: cnogo hook entries carry '_cnogo': true field. On install: add hooks with marker. On update: find and replace entries with marker. On uninstall: remove entries with marker. User hooks (no marker) are never touched. Claude Code ignores unknown fields in hook entries.
cnogo entries wrapped in '# >>> cnogo (do not edit this block)' / '# <<< cnogo'. Install adds block, update replaces block, uninstall removes block. User entries outside block are never touched.

decisions.decision: Context graph always ships as core feature
decisions.rationale: scripts/context/ is installed as part of every cnogo installation. Not optional. Includes all phases, walker, parser, model, storage.

decisions.decision: version.json in .cnogo/ tracks installed version and source commit
decisions.rationale: Contains version, source repo URL, source commit hash, installedAt, updatedAt. Enables upgrade detection, version comparison, and install health checks.

decisions.decision: install.sh handles all lifecycle operations via flags
decisions.rationale: Single script: install.sh [--update|--uninstall|--force] [--from <git-url>] <project-dir>. No separate uninstall.sh needed — manifest-based operations are all in one place.

decisions.decision: Update detects user modifications via hash comparison, backs up before overwriting
decisions.rationale: Compare current file hash vs manifest hash. If match: file unchanged by user, safe to overwrite. If mismatch: user modified it, backup to .cnogo/backup/<timestamp>/ then overwrite. Files removed in new version are removed from project. New files are added.

decisions.decision: Clean uninstall removes managed files, leaves user content
decisions.rationale: Remove managed files. Clean settings.json (remove _cnogo hooks). Clean .gitignore (remove cnogo block). Leave seeded files (CLAUDE.md, PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md). Leave scaffold directories (user has artifacts). Remove .cnogo/ entirely.

decisions.decision: Seeded files are created once and never overwritten
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md (root), PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, WORKFLOW.json are initialized from templates on first install. User edits them freely. Update never touches them. Uninstall leaves them. WORKFLOW.json gets new fields added with defaults on update (additive merge, not replace).

Entry-point scripts import _bootstrap as first import. _bootstrap.py adds .cnogo/ to sys.path so 'from scripts.memory import ...' resolves to .cnogo/scripts/memory/. All existing import patterns work unchanged. tests/conftest.py also adds .cnogo/ to sys.path.

decisions.decision: All commands, skills, hooks reference .cnogo/scripts/ paths
decisions.rationale: Hook paths in settings.json use $ROOT/.cnogo/scripts/. Command markdown uses 'python3 .cnogo/scripts/workflow_memory.py'. .claude/CLAUDE.md documents .cnogo/scripts/ paths. Consistent .cnogo/ prefix makes ownership visible.

constraints: Python stdlib only — no third-party dependencies
Claude Code requires .claude/commands/, .claude/agents/, .claude/skills/, .claude/settings.json at fixed paths
.cnogo/ starts with dot — invalid Python package name, _bootstrap.py resolves imports
install.sh must be a single bash script (no build tools required)
Uninstall must be clean — no orphaned files, no broken imports in target project
Context graph ships with every install (not optional)
Runtime databases (memory.db, graph.db) are gitignored, not tracked in manifest
issues.jsonl is git-tracked sync format, managed by memory engine
Self-hosting: cnogo installs into its own repo via the same mechanism as any other project
Same file layout in source repo and all installed repos — no transformation during install
settings.json is shared ownership — cnogo hooks coexist with user hooks
.gitignore is shared ownership — cnogo block coexists with user entries

resolvedQuestions.question: Python import path strategy for .cnogo/scripts/
resolvedQuestions.resolution: _bootstrap.py adds .cnogo/ to sys.path. Entry-point scripts import it first. All 'from scripts.memory import ...' patterns work unchanged.

resolvedQuestions.question: How do reference paths adapt to .cnogo/scripts/?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: All commands, skills, hooks updated to reference .cnogo/scripts/ paths. settings.json hooks use $ROOT/.cnogo/scripts/. Consistent prefix makes ownership visible.

resolvedQuestions.question: .gitignore management strategy
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Comment block markers (# >>> cnogo / # <<< cnogo) wrap cnogo entries in project .gitignore. Install adds, update replaces, uninstall removes the block.

managed.description: cnogo owns entirely. Overwrite on update. Remove on uninstall.

seeded.description: cnogo creates once from template. User owns after. Never overwrite. Leave on uninstall.

merged.description: Shared ownership. cnogo parts identified by markers. Merge on install/update. Remove cnogo parts on uninstall.

runtime.description: Created at runtime, gitignored. Recreate if missing. Remove on uninstall.

scaffold.description: Directory structure + .gitkeep files. Create once. Leave on uninstall (user has content).

.cnogo/.manifest.json: tracks ALL cnogo files across .cnogo/ and .claude/ with hashes and categories

scripts/._bootstrap.py: adds .cnogo/ to sys.path for import resolution
scripts/.memory/: memory engine package (13 modules, SQLite + JSONL sync)
scripts/.context/: context graph package (22 modules, AST parser, indexer, phases)

.claude/.settings.json: merged ownership — cnogo hooks marked with _cnogo:true

docs/planning/.WORKFLOW.json: seeded (user-owned, additive merge on update for new fields)

steps: Resolve source (clone repo or use local path)
Deploy seeded files (copy only if not exists)
Merge settings.json (add cnogo hooks with _cnogo marker)
Merge .gitignore (add cnogo block between comment markers)
Init runtime (create memory.db, index context graph)
Write manifest (record all files + hashes + version)
Read existing manifest (fail if not installed)
For each managed file: compare hash, backup if user-modified, overwrite
Remove files in old manifest but not in new version
Add new files not in old manifest
Additive merge WORKFLOW.json (add new fields with defaults)
Update manifest (new version, new hashes, new timestamp)
Report what was removed vs kept

reinstall.description: Uninstall + fresh install. Seeded files left in place.
